topic: Coding environments for AI agents — git worktree isolation, sandboxed execution, model routing, tool call limits, log management, session lifecycle

sources.title: git-worktree Documentation
keyInsights: Worktrees share object database but have private HEAD, index, and config
Per-worktree config enabled with extensions.worktreeConfig
git worktree add/remove/prune/repair lifecycle commands
git worktree lock prevents pruning for portable drives or long-running agents
Detached HEAD worktrees for ephemeral experiments with no branch pollution

sources.title: Claude Code: Create custom subagents
keyInsights: isolation: worktree field gives subagents their own git worktree, auto-cleaned if no changes
model field supports haiku/sonnet/opus aliases or full model IDs for routing
maxTurns field per subagent caps tool call rounds
Built-in Explore subagent uses Haiku with read-only tools for cheap codebase exploration
PreToolUse hooks enable conditional command validation before execution
SubagentStart/Stop hooks for setup/cleanup at lifecycle boundaries

sources.title: Claude Code: Orchestrate teams of Claude Code sessions
keyInsights: Agent teams use file locking to prevent race conditions on shared task queue
Each teammate has own context window; lead coordinates via shared task list + mailbox
Teammates do not inherit lead conversation history — spawn prompt must include context
Token cost scales linearly with number of teammates
TeammateIdle and TaskCompleted hooks enforce quality gates
Team config stored at ~/.claude/teams/<name>/config.json

sources.title: Claude Code: Common Workflows
keyInsights: claude --worktree <name> creates .claude/worktrees/<name> with branch worktree-<name>
Auto-generated name (random) when --worktree used without argument
Cleanup: auto-remove if no changes; prompt if commits exist
Add .claude/worktrees/ to .gitignore to prevent status noise
WorktreeCreate/WorktreeRemove hooks for non-git VCS systems

sources.title: OpenHands Runtime Architecture
keyInsights: Docker container per session with ActionExecutor managing bash, browser, Jupyter
Client-server model: backend sends actions via RESTful API, container returns observations
Image tagging: source tag (code hash) > lock tag (dep hash) > versioned tag
SANDBOX_VOLUME_OVERLAYS for copy-on-write overlay functionality
Plugin system extends capabilities asynchronously during runtime startup
File-locked port allocation for concurrent sessions

sources.title: SWE-agent: Usage and Environment
keyInsights: Ephemeral Docker container per task: docker run -i --rm sweagent/swe-agent:latest
Environment state via bash env vars: CURRENT_FILE, CURRENT_LINE, WINDOW
Interactive tools blocklisted: vim, python REPL, interactive git
Inline linting with flake8 after every edit call
Environment initialization takes ~1.5s before agent interaction begins

sources.title: OpenAI Codex CLI: Architecture
keyInsights: Three tiers: Suggest (read-only), Auto Edit (patches), Full Auto (sandboxed)
macOS Full Auto: Apple Seatbelt sandbox-exec, writable only $PWD/$TMPDIR/~/.codex
Linux Full Auto: Docker with iptables blocking all outbound except LLM API
Built in Rust (95.6%), shell-tool-mcp directory for MCP shell integration
History stores up to 1000 entries with sensitive pattern filtering

sources.title: Anthropic Go SDK: Tool Runner
keyInsights: Generic type parameter T for type-safe JSON unmarshaling of tool inputs
Three schema creation paths: raw bytes, struct reflection, direct schema param
Reflection-based: jsonschema.Reflector with AllowAdditionalProperties: false
Execute flow: receive json.RawMessage -> parse to T -> call handler -> return result
Error wrapping with fmt.Errorf for context propagation through tool call chain

sources.title: Go slog: Structured Logging Package
keyInsights: slog.NewJSONHandler outputs line-delimited JSON suitable for log aggregators
LevelVar enables runtime log level changes without restart
Groups organize attributes hierarchically to prevent key collisions across tools
LogValuer interface for custom types including secret redaction
MultiHandler (Go 1.26+) for simultaneous text (dev) and JSON (prod) output

sources.title: E2B: Cloud Sandboxes for AI Agents
keyInsights: Cloud-hosted Linux VMs as isolated sandboxes, SDK for JS/Python
State persists across runCode() calls within one sandbox instance
Lifecycle: create -> run (stateful) -> pause (snapshot) -> resume -> destroy
Supports filesystem, process management, Git, MCP server integration
Self-hostable on GCP (production-ready) and AWS (in progress)

sources.title: E2B Documentation Index
keyInsights: Sandbox lifecycle: create, pause, resume, destroy with webhook support
Git integration within sandboxes for agent coding sessions
MCP integration with 200+ tools for agent tool use
SSH and PTY terminal access for advanced agent interactions
BYOC (Bring Your Own Cloud) for enterprise deployments

sources.title: nsjail: Process Isolation Tool
keyInsights: Uses all 8 Linux namespaces: UTS, MOUNT, PID, IPC, NET, USER, CGROUPS, TIME
Seccomp-BPF filtering via Kafel policy language for fine-grained syscall control
Resource limits: --time_limit, --rlimit_as (memory), --rlimit_cpu
Lighter than Docker: no image management or daemon required
Ideal for per-command isolation inside an already-running container

sources.title: Docker Seccomp Security Profiles
keyInsights: Default profile blocks ~44 of 300+ syscalls; use allowlist model
Blocks: clone/unshare (namespace escape), ptrace (host inspection), module loading
Apply with: docker run --security-opt seccomp=/path/to/profile.json
Pair with capability dropping for defense-in-depth
Recommendation: use default profile unless specific needs require change

sources.title: Docker Run: Resource Constraints
keyInsights: --cpus=N for fractional CPU allocation; --cpuset-cpus for core pinning
-m/--memory for hard memory limit; --memory-reservation for soft limit
--memory-swap equal to --memory to disable swap for bounded execution
--network none for full network isolation; custom networks for inter-container comms
--cap-drop=ALL then add only required capabilities

sources.title: Firejail Sandbox Documentation
keyInsights: Desktop-focused process sandboxing using Linux user namespaces
Seccomp filtering, Linux capabilities restriction, network controls
1000+ pre-built profiles; transparent integration via firecfg
Best for sandboxing individual tool invocations rather than full runtime envs
Lighter than Docker for per-process isolation within a development environment

sources.title: Anthropic: Tool Use with Claude
keyInsights: Tool access is highest-leverage primitive: adds outsized capability gains on benchmarks
strict: true on tool definitions guarantees schema conformance (production agents)
Agentic loop: complete -> execute tools -> results back -> repeat until end_turn
Tool results go back as user-role messages to continue the conversation
stop_reason == 'end_turn' signals agent loop termination

sources.title: Claude Code: Settings
keyInsights: Four scope hierarchy: Managed > Local > Project > User
Project settings (.claude/settings.json) shared with team via git
Managed settings deployed via MDM/OS policies for enterprise
Permission modes: default, acceptEdits, dontAsk, bypassPermissions, plan
env field in settings.json for persistent environment variables

sources.title: Aider: FAQ
keyInsights: Model routing via edit format: wholefile for weak models, editblock for GPT-4o, universal diff for GPT-4 Turbo
Repo map disabled by default for weak models (overwhelming), force with --map-tokens
Reads latest file state on each message send, allowing mid-session file edits
.aiderignore files and --subtree-only flag for working-directory scoping
All changes committed to git with attribution tracking

sources.title: Aider: Usage Guide
keyInsights: Model switching via /model command during session or --model flag at startup
Automatic git commits for all AI changes with undo via /undo
Context management: add specific files rather than entire codebase
Related files auto-pulled for context without explicit addition
Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, local models via --model flag

sources.title: Plandex: AI Coding Agent
keyInsights: Cumulative diff review sandbox keeps changes isolated until developer runs apply
Full version control: plandex log, plandex rewind, plandex branch
Branch before rewind to prevent history loss
v2.0: default auto-reverts project files when rewinding past apply
Context file paths relative to plan creation directory for scoping

sources.title: Plandex: Plans Documentation
keyInsights: A plan = conversation + accumulated pending changes + loaded context
Plans archived (retained) or deleted permanently; plandex cd to switch
Working directory isolation: paths relative to plan creation directory
Plans can range from single-prompt to extensive multi-file sessions

sources.title: Plandex: Version Control
keyInsights: Every aspect version-controlled: context, prompts, responses, pending changes, model updates
plandex rewind [step count | commit id] for state restoration
No undo for rewind within a branch -- use branch strategy
plandex log for complete history; plandex convo for conversation history

sources.title: AutoGen: Code Executors
keyInsights: max_tool_iterations=10 configurable per agent for tool call limits
Docker executor for safe code execution in isolated containers
Local executor for trusted environments without container overhead
AgentTool pattern: agents as callable tools for delegation
Explicit init + close lifecycle: await agent.run() / await model_client.close()

sources.title: AutoGen Framework
keyInsights: Layered APIs: Extensions (low-level) + AgentChat (high-level, opinionated)
Multiple LLM backends via pluggable model client pattern
Agent-to-agent communication via AgentTool for delegation patterns
Supports OpenAI, Azure, local models via configuration

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK
keyInsights: Built-in agent loop handles tool invocation, result injection, and loop continuation
Handoffs as primary routing mechanism: agent-direct delegation
Sessions with multiple backends: SQLAlchemy, SQLite, encrypted
Agents as tools: callable via AgentTool for delegation patterns
Human-in-the-loop built into lifecycle management

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK: Handoffs
keyInsights: LLM-driven selection: register multiple handoffs, model decides which specialist to use
transfer_to_<agent_name> pattern for typed delegation
input_type adds metadata to chosen handoff for structured reasoning
Input filters transform history before receiving agent sees it
is_enabled: bool | function for runtime conditional handoffs
RECOMMENDED_PROMPT_PREFIX for consistent handoff instructions

sources.title: OpenAI Swarm
keyInsights: Handoff via function return: returning Agent object transfers execution
Stateless between calls; Response object carries messages + active_agent + context_vars
model_override parameter in client.run() for per-call model routing
Sequential tool call execution with error appended to history for recovery
Auto-converts Python functions to JSON Schema from docstrings + type hints

sources.title: LLM Powered Autonomous Agents (Lilian Weng)
keyInsights: Three components: Planning (decomposition + reflection), Memory (in-context + external), Tools
ReAct framework: Thought -> Action -> Observation cycle until completion
Short-term memory in context window; long-term via vector stores with ANN search
Critical limitations: finite context window, long-term planning difficulties, NL interface reliability
GPT-Engineer two-phase: clarification (resolve ambiguity) then implementation

sources.title: SWE-bench Benchmark
keyInsights: Top: Claude Opus 4.6 (high reasoning) 76.8% at 32.9 API calls, $0.75/issue
Gemini Flash 75.8% at 56.1 API calls, $0.36/issue -- cost efficiency matters
Reasoning-enhanced models outperform standard variants significantly
No single architecture dominates; efficiency tradeoffs vary by problem type
Tests resolve real GitHub issues against existing test suites

sources.title: Anthropic Go SDK
keyInsights: betatoolrunner for agent tool execution management
betaskill / betaskillversion for versioned skill definitions
messagebatch for batch processing in agent workflows
Thread-safe client design for concurrent agent operations
Go 1.22+ required; 447 commits, 1.1k dependents in production

sources.title: SWE-agent (Princeton NLP)
keyInsights: Single YAML config governs all agent behavior including tool limits
Docker container per task with ephemeral lifecycle (--rm)
Domain-specific tools: open, goto, search_dir, search_file, find_file, edit, create
Git patches track changes from base commit for review and integration
Environment initialization: ~1.5s container startup before agent begins

sources.title: OpenHands (formerly OpenDevin)
keyInsights: 77.6% SWE-bench score; supports 1000s of cloud agents
Software Agent SDK as composable Python library
Local GUI via REST API + single-page React app
Multi-user cloud with Slack, Jira, Linear integrations
MIT license for core; enterprise features separately licensed

sources.title: Cline (VS Code Extension)
keyInsights: Human-in-the-loop GUI approves every file change and terminal command
Direct IDE/filesystem access rather than sandboxed isolation
Workspace checkpoint functionality for compare and restore
Multi-provider model support: OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Bedrock, etc.
Human approval model trades autonomy for transparency

sources.title: Roo Code
keyInsights: Mode-based routing: Code (everyday editing), Architect (planning), Ask, Debug
Mode selection routes to appropriate tool access and behavior
Codebase indexing for local repository awareness
MCP server integration for external tool access

sources.title: smol developer
keyInsights: Modal for parallelizable code generation and dependency isolation
Three deployment modes: Git Repo, Library, API (Agent Protocol)
Sequential pipeline: plan() -> specify_file_paths() -> generate_code()
No native sandboxing for generated code -- relies on Modal's isolation

sources.title: Google OSS-Fuzz Gen
keyInsights: Containerized build isolation via Dockerfile + Dockerfile.cloudbuild-agent
Four-stage evaluation: compilability check before runtime testing
Multiple LLM backends: Vertex AI, OpenAI, Azure via abstraction layer
Batch processing via run_all_experiments.py with per-experiment results
Persistent bug tracking across execution cycles

sources.title: The 12-Factor App: Logs
keyInsights: Apps write to stdout only; never manage log files
Execution environment captures and routes log streams
Enables historical retrieval, trend analysis, alerting without app changes
Log routers (Logplex, Fluentd) handle persistence/rotation/indexing
Decouples app from infrastructure concerns for portability

sources.title: logrotate man page
keyInsights: size directive triggers rotation before time interval if file too large
delaycompress keeps yesterday's log uncompressed for active tail
maxage removes rotated logs older than N days
sharedscripts runs postrotate once for wildcarded patterns
postrotate/endscript for signaling apps to reopen log handles

sources.title: Claude Code GitHub Action
keyInsights: Runs entirely on your GitHub runner infrastructure
Only LLM API calls go to Anthropic (or Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry)
Structured JSON outputs automatically become GitHub Action outputs
Visual progress indicators with dynamically updated checkboxes
CI/CD integration pattern: PR reviews, issue triage, automated fixes

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK: Lifecycle Management
keyInsights: Multiple session backends: SQLAlchemy, SQLite, encrypted for different persistence needs
Handoffs enable agent-direct model routing without central orchestrator
Function tools: automatic schema generation from Python type hints via Pydantic
MCP server integration: works identically to standard function tools
Human-in-the-loop as first-class lifecycle concern

sources.title: Claude Code Overview
keyInsights: Reads codebase, edits files, runs commands across IDE, terminal, desktop, browser
CLAUDE.md for persistent project instructions across sessions
Auto-memory builds across sessions without explicit writing
Sub-agents spawnable for parallel task execution
GitHub Actions / GitLab CI integration for automated PR review

sources.title: E2B Documentation
keyInsights: Isolated sandboxes for safe AI code execution with full Linux VM
State persistence within sandbox across multiple runCode() calls
Snapshot-based pause/resume for long-running agent sessions
SDK available for JavaScript and Python
MCP integration enables 200+ tool access within sandboxes

sources.title: Plandex Installation Docs
keyInsights: Written in Go; WSL required for Windows
curl install or manual GitHub releases download
Build-from-source supported for Go developers
